data.narrative:
Reliable Robotics is a Series C–stage space and aerospace company that has raised $134M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer set of five same-stage private companies in Space & Aerospace—Axiom Space ($2.5B), Impulse Space ($1.8B), Varda Space Industries ($1.5B), Skyryse ($1.1B), and Isar Aerospace ($1.0B)—anchored against six public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) with a median price-to-sales multiple of 45.3x.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis, using the $134M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 1.7x Series C step-up multiplier, yields $228M. Peer-median analysis across the five same-sector, same-stage companies produces $1.5B, with valuations ranging from $1.0B to $2.5B. Public-comp anchoring is not applicable due to the absence of disclosed revenue data for the target. The three-method triangulation produces a range of $194M (low) to $1.7B (high), with a median of $864M across the two applicable methods.

The wide spread between stage-progression and peer-median results reflects divergence in methodology rather than a narrow consensus. Stage-progression relies on fundraising history alone, while peer-median reflects observed market pricing for comparable-stage companies. Key uncertainties include Reliable Robotics' revenue traction, capital efficiency relative to peers, and specific product-market positioning within the fragmented space sector.
